mission: Models + training (GCN encoder, MLP heads, STL/pairwise-MTL/all-task-MTL)

encoder_specification.name: gcn
encoder_specification.architecture: Linear(74->256) -> 3 x GCNConv(256,256) with ReLU+dropout=0.1 between -> mean pool -> Linear(256->256)

synthetic_fixture_adapter.rationale: fixture uses fake SMILES (smi_X_NN_M); RDKit cannot parse them, so A2's real featurizer is unusable. A3 provides a 4-node deterministic graph builder that mirrors the 74-dim atom-feature contract and includes a per-compound noisy label-leak channel so STL/MTL converge in <1s on CPU.
synthetic_fixture_adapter.production_path_unaffected: src/transfermtl/training/data.py reads PyG Data objects from outputs/cache/featurized/ as written by A2; the fixture adapter is test-only.

acceptance.wandb_logging_tag: tsh-mtl (via transfermtl.utils.logging.init); offline-safe no-op when wandb missing

deferred_for_real_data: STL Tox21 NR-AR val AUC > 0.75 — requires A2's split.parquet + featurization cache (not yet present at A3 PR time)
Pairwise MTL convergence on (NR-AR, NR-AR-LBD) Tox21 — same dependency
All-task MTL <20 GPU-min on H100 — same dependency, plus GPU access

out_of_scope_observation.test_validity_failure: tests/test_validity.py::test_meaningful_pair_4_conditions fails with 'assert (True is False)' on c4 setup. This file is owned by A4 (statistical core), not A3. Surfaced for A4's coordination.

notes_for_wave3: A6 should call `transfermtl.training.load_checkpoint(path)` and rebuild the model via `MultiHeadModel(GCNEncoder(), task_names)` then `model.load_state_dict(bundle.model_state)` — checkpoints store cfg_dict + seed + git_sha for reproducibility.
Predictions parquet rows are one-per-(smiles, task); for STL there are n_test rows, for pairwise MTL there are 2*n_test rows.
PyG `Data.y` is shaped (1, n_tasks); after PyG batching `batch.y` is (B, n_tasks). NaN entries are masked by the loss functions in stl/pairwise_mtl/all_task_mtl.
transfermtl.training.load_pyg_dataset is the single entrypoint for turning A2's split parquet + featurization cache into a list of PyG Data objects with .y and .smi attached.
